content.text:
Done. Here's a summary of what was implemented across 20 files (15 new, 5 modified):

**Token palette & layout** — `itemLibrary.component.scss` fully rewritten with CSS custom properties (`--brand`, `--accent`, `--paper`, `--bg`, etc.), Hanken Grotesk / IBM Plex Mono, `lib-pop` keyframe, flex-based layout that eliminates the old `calc(100vh - ...)` height hacks.

**Command bar** — single `div.command-bar` replaces the `mat-toolbar`. Contains: brand tile → Create Item (terracotta) → search → Division select → Display popover (Layout/View/Density/Columns) → saved-views select → layout-dropdown → Export icon → Reset. Field-buttons with active state (indigo bg/border/text).

**Cell renderers** (3 new AG-Grid components):
- `ItemThumbnailComponent` — real `pic.Thumbnail` image; falls back to property-hashed gradient tile + SVG glyph + item-code overlay. Click opens the image editor.
- `StatusPillComponent` — 8-stage tone map (concept/design/submitted/approved/sampling/production/shelf/discont) → colored pill with dot.
- `PropertyCellComponent` — consistent-hue color dot + resolved property name.

**Layouts** (2 new components + template switching):
- **Split workspace** — `SplitRailComponent` (372px fixed rail): large image, status pill, 10-field 2-col grid, Open item footer. Activated by single row click.
- **Gallery view** — `GalleryComponent`: `auto-fill minmax(210px, 1fr)` card grid with real/fallback thumbnails, status pills, property dots, checkboxes, double-click to open.
- **Filmstrip hover preview** — absolute-positioned 234px card that appears at the row's Y position on `rowMouseEnter`.

**Preserved** — server-side datasource, saved-view save/restore, Division column logic, Export/Excel, Archive context menu, cell notes, bulk license.

file.content:
# AGENTS.md — Designflow Frontend: Primary Developer Guide

Read this file in full before touching any code. It is the authoritative orientation document for any developer or AI session working on this repository.

---

## 1. What this project is

Designflow is a product-lifecycle-management (PLM) web application used by sourcing, merchandising, licensing, and production teams to manage the full journey of a product from concept to retail. The core workflows are: **RFQ** (request for quote — collect vendor pricing and compute landed-cost and margin chains), **Item Library** (product master data), **Standardized Products**, **Art Piece** management, and **Production/Licensing Tracking**. This repository is the Angular frontend only; the backend REST API is a separate service at `api.designflow.app`. The business-critical outcome is correct margin and landed-cost calculations in the RFQ grid — errors there directly affect buy prices and retailer quotes.

---

## 2. Multi-model AI note

There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.

`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.

When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.

---

## 2a. Documentation map: what to read for each task

Always start with this file (`AGENTS.md`). Then load other docs **only when relevant** — do
not ingest every `.md`.

| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
|---|---|---|
| Quick orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | deep `docs/` |
| RFQ margin / landed-cost / pricing | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/rfq-math.md`, `src/app/pages/rfq/README.md` | `docs/deployment.md` |
| Work in the RFQ module | `AGENTS.md`, `src/app/pages/rfq/README.md`, `docs/architecture.md` if design affected | unrelated module docs |
| Sample Tracking module | `AGENTS.md` (§3, §7, §8 Sample rows), `docs/architecture.md` (Sample Tracking section) | RFQ math docs |
| Add/change config, env vars, API base URLs | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/configuration.md` | `docs/deployment.md` unless prod runtime changes |
| Local setup, run, test, lint, debug | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/development.md` | `docs/deployment.md` |
| Deploy / Docker / Cloud Build / nginx / rollback | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/deployment.md`, `docs/configuration.md` | local-only dev docs |
| AG Grid column/grid work | `AGENTS.md` (§11 quirks), `docs/architecture.md` (AG Grid patterns), the feature's `helpers/ag-grid/*.config.ts` | unrelated module docs |
| Investigate a bug/incident | `AGENTS.md` (§14 incident log), the area's doc, `HANDOFF.md` | unrelated folder READMEs |
| Licensing tracking ↔ poppim PM integration | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/licensing-tracking-poppim-integration.md`, `docs/licensing-poppim-phase0-bridge.md`, `HANDOFF.md` | RFQ math, deployment docs |
| Continue unfinished work | `AGENTS.md`, **`HANDOFF.md`** (present — required), docs it names | docs outside the handoff scope |
| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | other docs unless needed |
| Docs-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, affected `docs/` | source except to verify |

`HANDOFF.md` currently exists (covers prior notifications/RAG follow-ups) and is required
reading for continuation work. Update this table whenever a doc is added, removed, or repurposed.

---

## 3. Repository / package structure

| Path | What it is |
|---|---|
| `src/app/pages/` | **Our code** — all feature modules (RFQ, item library, tracking, admin, etc.) |
| `src/app/helpers/` | **Our code** — services, models, HTTP interceptors, AG Grid column configs |
| `src/app/components/` | **Our code** — shared reusable components |
| `src/app/custom-layout/` | **Our code** — shell layout wrapping all authenticated routes |
| `src/app/app-routing.module.ts` | **Our code** — all route definitions and guards |
| `src/app/app.module.ts` | **Our code** — root NgModule, providers, interceptors |
| `src/environments/` | **Our code** — per-environment API base URL config |
| `src/@vex/` | **Framework code** — Vex UI/layout framework (committed into the repo, not a package). Modifiable only with specific justification; avoid casual edits. |

content.content:
1	# Designflow Feature Dependency Matrix
2	
3	This matrix maps major user-facing and system-facing features to frontend
4	modules, API gateway prefixes, backend services, route groups, shared data, and
5	external dependencies.
6	
7	The target audience is an engineer trying to answer: "If this feature changes,
8	which repos and services should I inspect first?"
9	
10	## Matrix
11	
12	| Feature | Frontend area / service consumer | BFF prefix | Primary service | Main route groups | Data touched | External dependencies |
13	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
14	| Authentication: password / legacy account flows | `pages/auth`, `MainService`, auth guards/interceptors | `/api/core` | `designflow-backend` | `/signup`, `/verifyAuthToken`, `/forgotPassw`, `/changePassword`, `/findUserLoginInfo` | `users`, `vendor`, auth token tables | Brevo for email flows |
15	| Authentication: Microsoft SSO | `AzureSsoService`, `auth-redirect.html`, auth pages | `/api/core` | `designflow-backend` | `/auth/azure` | `users`, `vendor` | Microsoft Azure AD JWKS |
16	| JWT propagation and service ingress | `UserAuthInterceptor` | all `/api/:service` paths | `designflow-bff` plus all private services | `/api/:service/*` | none directly | Google Cloud Run OIDC |
17	| Role-based navigation and route protection | Angular route guards, `app.component.ts`, toolbar/menu | all | frontend plus service auth middleware | `authRole([...])` in service routers | `users`, `Roles`, `RolePermissions`, `UIElements` | none |

# Designflow Feature Dependency Matrix

This matrix maps major user-facing and system-facing features to frontend
modules, API gateway prefixes, backend services, route groups, shared data, and
external dependencies.

The target audience is an engineer trying to answer: "If this feature changes,
which repos and services should I inspect first?"

## Matrix

| Feature | Frontend area / service consumer | BFF prefix | Primary service | Main route groups | Data touched | External dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Authentication: password / legacy account flows | `pages/auth`, `MainService`, auth guards/interceptors | `/api/core` | `designflow-backend` | `/signup`, `/verifyAuthToken`, `/forgotPassw`, `/changePassword`, `/findUserLoginInfo` | `users`, `vendor`, auth token tables | Brevo for email flows |
| Authentication: Microsoft SSO | `AzureSsoService`, `auth-redirect.html`, auth pages | `/api/core` | `designflow-backend` | `/auth/azure` | `users`, `vendor` | Microsoft Azure AD JWKS |
| JWT propagation and service ingress | `UserAuthInterceptor` | all `/api/:service` paths | `designflow-bff` plus all private services | `/api/:service/*` | none directly | Google Cloud Run OIDC |
| Role-based navigation and route protection | Angular route guards, `app.component.ts`, toolbar/menu | all | frontend plus service auth middleware | `authRole([...])` in service routers | `users`, `Roles`, `RolePermissions`, `UIElements` | none |
| Admin impersonation | `ImpersonationService`, Manage Users UI, toolbar banner | `/api/core` | `designflow-backend` | `/api/admin/impersonate/:userId` | `users`, `vendor`, `Factory` | JWT signing keys |
| RFQ grid load | `pages/rfq`, `MainService` | `/api/core` | `designflow-backend` | `/getAllItem`, `/getRFQViewlayout`, `/getCustomGridLayout`, `/getGridDefaultView` | `RFQItem`, `RFQVendor`, `RFQGroup`, `GridLayout`, `GridViewState`, `viewlayout` | none |
| RFQ item editing | `pages/rfq`, AG Grid value setters | `/api/core` | `designflow-backend` | `/updateRfqItem`, `/addRfqItem`, `/deleteRFQItem`, `/archiveRFQItem` | `RFQItem`, `RFQGroup`, audit/log tables | none |
| RFQ vendor quote editing | RFQ detail renderer, vendor screens | `/api/core` | `designflow-backend` | `/updateVendor`, `/addingVendor`, `/deleteRFQItemVendor`, `/selectedVendor`, `/addMultipleVendor` | `RFQVendor`, `vendor`, `Factory` | Brevo for notifications/emails |
| RFQ quote requests and re-quotes | RFQ grid/detail, vendor flows | `/api/core` | `designflow-backend` | `/sendPriceReq`, `/sendMultiplePriceReq`, `/requestRequote`, `/keepPrice` | `RFQVendor`, `quote_auth_token`, `user_notification` | Brevo |

11	### What it does

# HANDOFF — Remaining open items

Open items that need continuation context are listed below.

---

## Factory bulk offer upload — **IMPLEMENTED, NOT YET COMMITTED** (updated 2026-06-11)

A vendor-only "Upload Offers" feature was fully implemented across both `designflow-frontend` and `designflow-backend` in this session. All code sits as unstaged changes on branch `sandbox-albert` in both repos.

### What it does

Factory/vendor users see an **Upload Offers** button (`@if (role === 'vendor')`) in the RFQ toolbar. Clicking it opens a 3-stage dialog:

1. **Form** — download a pre-formatted `.xlsx` template (pre-filled with factory name, date, Customer dropdown, Price Terms dropdown, 50 tall data rows with yellow image cells); name the RFQ group; drop-zone to select the completed file
2. **Uploading** — spinner while the API processes the file
3. **Result** — list of created items (price code + description) and any row-level errors (bilingual English · Chinese)

On the backend, the import creates:
- One `RFQGroup` (named by the factory)
- One `RFQItem` per non-empty data row — price code `foff-XXXX` (global sequential; padded to 4 digits)
- One `RFQVendor` quote record per row (linked to the uploading factory's `factory_id` from JWT; `req_status = 2` = quoted; lands at @factories step)
- Embedded product images extracted from the xlsx and uploaded to DigitalOcean Spaces via `uploadBufferToSpaces`

### Files changed — frontend

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/app/pages/rfq/factory-bulk-upload/factory-bulk-upload-dialog.component.ts` | **NEW** — `standalone: false`, `stage: 'form'|'uploading'|'result'`, JWT vendor name pre-fill, drag-and-drop file zone, `firstValueFrom()` calls |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/factory-bulk-upload/factory-bulk-upload-dialog.component.html` | **NEW** — 3-stage template |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/factory-bulk-upload/factory-bulk-upload-dialog.component.scss` | **NEW** — drop zone, result, error banner styles |
| `src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts` | Added `getRFQFactoryTemplate()` (GET → blob) and `importFactoryRFQ(formData)` (POST → multipart) |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts` | Added `onFactoryBulkUpload()` — decodes JWT for vendor name, opens dialog, refreshes grid + snackbar on success |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.html` | Added `@if (role === 'vendor')` Upload Offers button in toolbar |
| `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.module.ts` | Added `FactoryBulkUploadDialogComponent` to `declarations` |

### Files changed — backend (see `designflow-backend/HANDOFF.md`)

`helpers/rfqBulkImport.js` (new), `helpers/digitalocean.js`, `helpers/multer.js`, `models/lib.model.js`, `controllers/main.controller.js`, `routes/lib.router.js`.

### Current state

- All changes are on `sandbox-albert`, **unstaged** (no `git add`, no `git commit`)
- `develop` was merged into `sandbox-albert` again on 2026-06-11 across the child repos; local uncommitted frontend/backend work was preserved.
- 2026-06-11 UX hardening added bilingual disabled-state/error feedback around this workflow: `Import Offers` now explains missing offer group name and/or `.xlsx` file; download/upload/file-type errors are user-visible in English and Simplified Mandarin.

input.content:
# Item Library Redesign

The Item Library page (`pages/itemLibrary`) was re-skinned and extended to make
product imagery prominent while keeping the dense, server-side AG-Grid table that
power users rely on. This document covers the redesign's components, shared
helpers, and runtime toggles. For the original design intent see the design
handoff README that accompanied this work.

## Design tokens

The page uses CSS custom properties defined on the component `:host` in
`itemLibrary.component.scss`, lifted from the approved Item Detail palette:

| Token | Value | Use |
|---|---|---|
| `--ink` / `--ink-2..4` | `#1c1b22` … `#a8a6af` | text shades |
| `--line` / `--line-2` | `#e7e3dd` / `#f0ece6` | borders / hairlines |
| `--paper` / `--bg` / `--chip` | `#ffffff` / `#f5f3ef` / `#f1eee9` | surfaces |
| `--brand` / `--brand-700` / `--brand-50` | `#3f3d86` / `#302e63` / `#ecebf6` | indigo — actions, selection |
| `--accent` / `--accent-700` / `--accent-50` | `#c2662f` / `#9a4f23` / `#f7ece2` | terracotta — Create Item |

Typography: Hanken Grotesk (sans) + IBM Plex Mono (IDs/codes/counts).

## Command bar

The former multi-row `mat-toolbar` is consolidated into a single
`div.command-bar`: brand tile + title, Create Item, search (`dflow-search-tag`),
Division `mat-select`, a **Display** popover (Layout / View / Density / Columns),
saved-views `mat-select`, the `layout-dropdown` columns control, Export, and
Reset (`dflow-reset-aggrid-filters`). The Display popover's Columns checklist is
wired to AG-Grid column visibility via `setColumnsVisible`.

## Layouts, views, density

All three are runtime toggles over the same datasource, filters, sort, and
selection — set in the Display popover and held as component state:

- **Layout** — `filmstrip` (full-width table + hover preview card) or `split`
  (table + persistent 372px preview rail). State: `layout`.
- **View** — `table` or `gallery` (card grid). State: `view`.
- **Density** — `comfortable` (row height 64) or `compact` (46). State:
  `density`; `getRowHeight` reads it and `resetRowHeights()` is called on change.

Default visible columns: 11 of 13 — Reference # and Design # are hidden by
default (`hide: true` in `item.library.config.ts`).

## Components

| Component | Selector | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemThumbnailComponent` | `app-item-thumbnail` | AG-Grid cell renderer (`itemThumbnail`): real `pic.Thumbnail`, falls back to a property-hued gradient tile + category glyph + item-code chip; click opens the image editor |
| `StatusPillComponent` | `app-status-pill` | AG-Grid cell renderer (`statusPill`): status → tone-mapped pill |
| `PropertyCellComponent` | `app-property-cell` | AG-Grid cell renderer (`propertyCell`): property color dot + name |
| `SplitRailComponent` | `app-split-rail` | Split-workspace preview rail bound to the clicked row (image, status, full field grid, Open item) |
| `GalleryComponent` | `app-item-gallery` | Card-grid view over the same `rowData`; selection + double-click to open |

The renderers are registered in `frameworkComponents` in
`itemLibrary.component.ts` and the column config points the relevant columns at
them in `helpers/ag-grid/item.library.config.ts`.

## Shared helpers — `item-library-shared.ts`

Pure, framework-free helpers shared by the components so the status tones and
property hues stay in lockstep (and are unit-testable in isolation):

- `resolveStatusTone(status)` — case/whitespace-insensitive status → `PillTone`,
  defaulting to the `concept` tone. Backed by `STATUS_TONES` + `STATUS_KEY_MAP`.
- `stringToHue(str)` / `propertyHue(item)` — deterministic 0–359 hue so a
  property always renders the same color across tile, dot, and gallery card.
- `tileGradient` / `tileBorderColor` / `glyphColor` / `propertyDotColor` — hue → CSS.
- `resolveThumbSrc(item)` — best available image URL or `null`.
- `buildRailFields(item)` / `railItemName` / `railItemMeta` — split-rail content.

## Tests

`item-library-shared.spec.ts` covers the helpers exhaustively (tone mapping,
hue range/determinism, rail field derivation, thumb fallback). Each component
also has a behavioral spec: `gallery`, `split-rail`, `status-pill`, and
`property-cell`. Run with `npx jest src/app/pages/itemLibrary`.

## What is preserved

Server-side row model (`cacheBlockSize=100`, `maxBlocksInCache=10`), saved-view
save/restore, Division column-visibility logic, Excel export, cell notes, the
archive context menu, and `dflow-grid-active-chips` are all unchanged.

toolUseResult.content:
# Item Library Redesign

The Item Library page (`pages/itemLibrary`) was re-skinned and extended to make
product imagery prominent while keeping the dense, server-side AG-Grid table that
power users rely on. This document covers the redesign's components, shared
helpers, and runtime toggles. For the original design intent see the design
handoff README that accompanied this work.

## Design tokens

The page uses CSS custom properties defined on the component `:host` in
`itemLibrary.component.scss`, lifted from the approved Item Detail palette:

| Token | Value | Use |
|---|---|---|
| `--ink` / `--ink-2..4` | `#1c1b22` … `#a8a6af` | text shades |
| `--line` / `--line-2` | `#e7e3dd` / `#f0ece6` | borders / hairlines |
| `--paper` / `--bg` / `--chip` | `#ffffff` / `#f5f3ef` / `#f1eee9` | surfaces |
| `--brand` / `--brand-700` / `--brand-50` | `#3f3d86` / `#302e63` / `#ecebf6` | indigo — actions, selection |
| `--accent` / `--accent-700` / `--accent-50` | `#c2662f` / `#9a4f23` / `#f7ece2` | terracotta — Create Item |

Typography: Hanken Grotesk (sans) + IBM Plex Mono (IDs/codes/counts).

## Command bar

The former multi-row `mat-toolbar` is consolidated into a single
`div.command-bar`: brand tile + title, Create Item, search (`dflow-search-tag`),
Division `mat-select`, a **Display** popover (Layout / View / Density / Columns),
saved-views `mat-select`, the `layout-dropdown` columns control, Export, and
Reset (`dflow-reset-aggrid-filters`). The Display popover's Columns checklist is
wired to AG-Grid column visibility via `setColumnsVisible`.

## Layouts, views, density

All three are runtime toggles over the same datasource, filters, sort, and
selection — set in the Display popover and held as component state:

- **Layout** — `filmstrip` (full-width table + hover preview card) or `split`
  (table + persistent 372px preview rail). State: `layout`.
- **View** — `table` or `gallery` (card grid). State: `view`.
- **Density** — `comfortable` (row height 64) or `compact` (46). State:
  `density`; `getRowHeight` reads it and `resetRowHeights()` is called on change.

Default visible columns: 11 of 13 — Reference # and Design # are hidden by
default (`hide: true` in `item.library.config.ts`).

## Components

| Component | Selector | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemThumbnailComponent` | `app-item-thumbnail` | AG-Grid cell renderer (`itemThumbnail`): real `pic.Thumbnail`, falls back to a property-hued gradient tile + category glyph + item-code chip; click opens the image editor |
| `StatusPillComponent` | `app-status-pill` | AG-Grid cell renderer (`statusPill`): status → tone-mapped pill |
| `PropertyCellComponent` | `app-property-cell` | AG-Grid cell renderer (`propertyCell`): property color dot + name |
| `SplitRailComponent` | `app-split-rail` | Split-workspace preview rail bound to the clicked row (image, status, full field grid, Open item) |
| `GalleryComponent` | `app-item-gallery` | Card-grid view over the same `rowData`; selection + double-click to open |

The renderers are registered in `frameworkComponents` in
`itemLibrary.component.ts` and the column config points the relevant columns at
them in `helpers/ag-grid/item.library.config.ts`.

## Shared helpers — `item-library-shared.ts`

Pure, framework-free helpers shared by the components so the status tones and
property hues stay in lockstep (and are unit-testable in isolation):

- `resolveStatusTone(status)` — case/whitespace-insensitive status → `PillTone`,
  defaulting to the `concept` tone. Backed by `STATUS_TONES` + `STATUS_KEY_MAP`.
- `stringToHue(str)` / `propertyHue(item)` — deterministic 0–359 hue so a
  property always renders the same color across tile, dot, and gallery card.
- `tileGradient` / `tileBorderColor` / `glyphColor` / `propertyDotColor` — hue → CSS.
- `resolveThumbSrc(item)` — best available image URL or `null`.
- `buildRailFields(item)` / `railItemName` / `railItemMeta` — split-rail content.

## Tests

`item-library-shared.spec.ts` covers the helpers exhaustively (tone mapping,
hue range/determinism, rail field derivation, thumb fallback). Each component
also has a behavioral spec: `gallery`, `split-rail`, `status-pill`, and
`property-cell`. Run with `npx jest src/app/pages/itemLibrary`.

## What is preserved

Server-side row model (`cacheBlockSize=100`, `maxBlocksInCache=10`), saved-view
save/restore, Division column-visibility logic, Excel export, cell notes, the
archive context menu, and `dflow-grid-active-chips` are all unchanged.

The Item Library page (`pages/itemLibrary`) was re-skinned to the Item Detail
token palette and extended with image-forward layouts. Committed and pushed to

### What shipped

  a **Display** popover for Layout / View / Density / Columns.

  (card-grid view) over the same server-side datasource.

content.content:
{"body":"## Item Detail page redesign — Direction A ("two-column dossier")

Recreates the approved redesign of the **item detail** Main tab (`/apps/itemDetail/:id`) in the real Angular + Material environment (the prototype's inline styles were treated as a value spec, not shipped). Build green (`ng build --configuration sandbox`, 0 errors); itemDetail jest specs **11/11**.

- **Page header** — Active/division status pills, display name `h1`, mono identity row (Style # / Reference # / Art # → art-piece link); actions: *Edit Details* (existing edit flow) + the existing ColdLion `Request a style number` / `Update Item Details`.
- **Colored primary tab bar** — custom Main/Licensing/Calendar tabs with tinted icon badges (replaces the plain `mat-tab-group`; div-9 still hides Licensing; `?tab=licensing` deep-link preserved).
- **Production timeline band** — 5-milestone stepper with done/current/todo state computed from the item's real date fields + accent "Due" pill.
- **Gallery + At a glance**, **Item Attributes** 4-column colored field grid with a First/Second/Third segmented control (MG-code chips), and a **Product Team / Tech Pack / Tags / Comments** bottom row.
- **Tokens** ported to scoped SCSS (`.df-redesign`): colors, 8-hue palette, pill tones, type scale, spacing; Hanken Grotesk + IBM Plex Mono added to `index.html`; responsive collapse at 1180/720px.

### Data wiring & constraints honored
- Bound to real fields (`item_displ_descr_name`, `item_num_id`, `ref_num`, `dsn_ref_num`, `udf_merchgroup01-15`, dates, vendor, assignments). MG01-MG15 mapping confirmed against the real `getMerchGroup`/`mgTypeDesc` schema; **division-9** Big Theme / Little Theme / Art Type variant wired.
- Edit mode, product-team assignment saves, image upload, techpack dialogs, tags editor, comments, and the Licensing (`license-tracking`) / Calendar (`vex-calendar`) tabs preserved.
- Re-added the **Cust Designed** field above For Cust; updated `itemDetail-for-cust-placement.spec` to the new markup and added `itemDetail-redesign-helpers.spec` (timeline/initials/team/division getters).

### Notes / follow-ups
- The design's bespoke **Licensing** (StageTracker/FeedbackTable) and **Calendar** (MiniCalendar/agenda) tab layouts are not applied — those are separate existing components and the handoff sequences them after A·Main.
- Not yet eyeballed against a running item in the browser.

---

## RFQ page polish (design handoff)

Implements four approved UX/visual improvements on the RFQ module, one PR-sized commit each. Full RFQ spec suite green (96 tests across `rfq.component.spec`, `rfq.grid.config.spec`, `rfq.detail.grid.config.spec`) and `tsc --noEmit` clean.

1. **Detail sub-grid flex sizing** (`rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` + live `detail-cell-renderer`) — Note is the sole flexing column (`flex:1` + `minWidth`, no hard width); priced/lookup columns get minWidth floors + right-alignment; Price next to Name. Gapless at any width; no `sizeColumnsToFit` alongside flex.
2. **Density toggle** (`rfq.component.ts`/`.html`) — Compact/Standard/Comfortable row+header heights via `setGridOption` + `resetRowHeights`, persisted to `localStorage['rfq:density']`, restored on grid ready. 3-segment `mat-button-toggle` in the toolbar.
3. **Frozen identity + seam** (`rfq.grid.config.ts` + RFQ scss) — `pic`/`desc` pinned left (design widths 56/280); seam (rule + shadow) on the last pinned-left header/cell; brand pink-soft active-row highlight spanning the seam.
4. **Collapsible pricing scenario column groups** (`rfq.component.ts` + scss) — Gen/Lic × FOB/POE/WHSE/MDDP leaves wrapped into AG-Grid column groups (Price-for-Sales always visible; Sell/Margin/Royalty `columnGroupShow:'open'`; `marryChildren`); toolbar expand/collapse-all via `setColumnGroupOpened`; group-band header tints.

### Constraints honored
- All row-data access stays on `getMasterFiledName()`; `this.colHeaders` stays flat — grouping is layered onto `columnDefs` only.
- Did not touch the fill-handle double-click blocker, native undo, or the detail default price sort.
- No column has both `flex` and `sizeColumnsToFit`.

### Notes / follow-ups
- `groupDefaultExpanded` intentionally left as-is (that controls *row* grouping; column-group collapse is handled by `columnGroupShow`).
- Column-group open/closed state is session-only; persisting it in named-view layouts (`layout-dropdown`) is a deliberate follow-up.
- Verified via tests + typecheck; not yet eyeballed in a running browser.

---

## (Earlier in this branch) poppim PM integration analysis + AG Grid 35.3 deps gotcha

Documentation-only. Records durable knowledge from a session that (a) analyzed how DesignFlow's `licensing_tracking` module overlaps with the **poppim** PM system, and (b) hit a phantom test failure worth flagging for future sessions.

- **`docs/licensing-tracking-poppim-integration.md` (new)** — `licensing_tracking` vs poppim overlap; separate clouds/DBs, no existing integration; chosen direction: DesignFlow stays system of record, poppim consumes a read-only copy; shared key is the DesignFlow `item_num_id` embedded in poppim's `product.name`. 9,293 distinct item-nos across 58% of 16,534 poppim rows.
- **`AGENTS.md`** — §14 incident-log entry: phantom `rfq.component.spec` TS errors were stale `node_modules` after a `develop` merge bumped AG Grid 35.2.1 → 35.3; fix is `yarn install`.
- **`HANDOFF.md`** — shared-key verification handoff (blocked on a read-only DB export), with the exact A/B/C queries and next action.

content.text:
Found it. The Cloud Build failed on a TypeScript error my local check missed (my `Select-String "itemLibrary"` filter hid it — the wrapped error line didn't contain the filename):

```
TS2353: 'onRowMouseEnter' does not exist in type 'GridOptions<any>'
```

`onRowMouseEnter`/`onRowMouseLeave` aren't valid AG-Grid GridOptions. The correct event is `onCellMouseOver`, with row-exit handled via a DOM `mouseleave` on the grid container. Let me fix it.

content.text:
Fixed and confirmed green.

## What happened

"Commits failed" was the **CI Cloud Build** failing on PR #124 — not the git commits (those succeeded and were on the remote). The build failed with:

```
TS2353: 'onRowMouseEnter' does not exist in type 'GridOptions<any>'
```

**Root cause:** I used `onRowMouseEnter`/`onRowMouseLeave` for the filmstrip hover preview, but those aren't valid AG-Grid `GridOptions` callbacks. My earlier local verification missed it because I filtered the typecheck output with `Select-String "itemLibrary"` — the wrapped error line didn't contain the filename on the same line, so the grep hid it. A lesson: I ran a filtered partial check instead of the real build.

**The fix** (commit `9459a23d`):
- Switched the hover trigger to the supported `onCellMouseOver` event (guarded to only re-fire when the hovered row changes)
- Clear the preview via a DOM `(mouseleave)` on the grid-region container

**Verification this time** — I ran the *actual* CI build command (`ng build --configuration sandbox`) locally and confirmed:
- The `onCellMouseOver`/`onRowMouseEnter` error is gone
- My new files (shared helper + 5 components) have **zero** errors
- The only remaining itemLibrary line flagged locally is the pre-existing `notesDataSource` (cell-notes feature, identical in rfq/art-piece/licensing/prod tracking) — an artifact of my stale local `node_modules`, not present in CI's fresh install

Then I pushed and **watched CI to confirm rather than assert**: build `54b6666b` now **passes** (6m35s). PR #124 is green and open.
